Sample.Title: NS-R1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: 1-3 x107 CDK1as wild type cells/sample were treated with 1NM-PP1 (final concentration: 2 μ M) for 10 h, after which over 95% were in G2 (Fig.S14). G2-arrested cells were collected prior to 1NM-PP1-washout. To release cells from the G2-arrest, 1NM-PP1 was washed out by collecting the cells by centrifugation (1300 rpm, 2-5 min, depending on the volume of cultured cells, at RT) and suspending the cells in fresh media (10-20 ml). Cells were washed twice over a period of 10-20 min (depending on the number of cultures being processed simultaneously). These cells were suspended in fresh media and divided into flasks (one flask for each time point, typically 1-3 x107 cells per flask) and returned to the incubator (counted asTime=0 min). At each time point (0, 2.5, 5, 7.5, 10, 30, 45, 60, 75 min), a flask was removed from the incubator and cells with media were transferred to a 50 ml falcon tube, which was topped up with fresh media to 45 ml.
Channel.Extract-Protocol: 3 ml of 16% formaldehyde solution was added to the 50 ml tube to obtain a final concentration of 1%. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 10 min for cross-linking. 2.5 ml of 2.5 M glycine was then added to the tube to stop cross-linking. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 5 min, and then on ice for at least 15 min. Subsequently, cells were collected by centrifugation (2000 rpm, 10 min), and the cell pellet was frozen on dry ice and then stored at -80°C. HiC was performed as in Belton et. al, 2012, http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1046202312001168?via%3Dihub

Sample.Data-Processing: cMapping Pipeline is publicly availalble at https://github.com/dekkerlab/cMapping This pipeline is describe in the publication by Lajoie et. Al., http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1046202314003582?via%3Dihub Cooler files were obtained from validpairs files. cooler is publicly available at https://github.com/mirnylab/cooler Genome_build: galGal5 Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: validpairs This format gives a list of the interacting pairs of loci in the genome, cooler: chromosome interractions in sparse form. For details, refer to https://github.com/mirnylab/cooler

At each time point (0, 2.5, 5, 7.5, 10, 30, 45, 60, 75 min), a flask was removed from the incubator and cells with media were transferred to a 50 ml falcon tube, which was topped up with fresh media to 45 ml.
Channel.Extract-Protocol: 3 ml of 16% formaldehyde solution was added to the 50 ml tube to obtain a final concentration of 1%. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 10 min for cross-linking. 2.5 ml of 2.5 M glycine was then added to the tube to stop cross-linking. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 5 min, and then on ice for at least 15 min. Subsequently, cells were collected by centrifugation (2000 rpm, 10 min), and the cell pellet was frozen on dry ice and then stored at -80°C. HiC was performed as in Belton et. al, 2012, http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1046202312001168?via%3Dihub

Subsequently, cells were collected by centrifugation (2000 rpm, 10 min), and the cell pellet was frozen on dry ice and then stored at -80°C. HiC was performed as in Belton et. al, 2012, http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1046202312001168?via%3Dihub

Sample.Title: 20150812-G2-R1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: 1-3 x107 CDK1as wild type cells/sample were treated with 1NM-PP1 (final concentration: 2 μ M) for 10 h, after which over 95% were in G2 (Fig.S14). G2-arrested cells were collected prior to 1NM-PP1-washout. To release cells from the G2-arrest, 1NM-PP1 was washed out by collecting the cells by centrifugation (1300 rpm, 2-5 min, depending on the volume of cultured cells, at RT) and suspending the cells in fresh media (10-20 ml). Cells were washed twice over a period of 10-20 min (depending on the number of cultures being processed simultaneously). These cells were suspended in fresh media and divided into flasks (one flask for each time point, typically 1-3 x107 cells per flask) and returned to the incubator (counted asTime=0 min). Channel.Extract-Protocol: 3 ml of 16% formaldehyde solution was added to the 50 ml tube to obtain a final concentration of 1%. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 10 min for cross-linking. 2.5 ml of 2.5 M glycine was then added to the tube to stop cross-linking. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 5 min, and then on ice for at least 15 min. Subsequently, cells were collected by centrifugation (2000 rpm, 10 min), and the cell pellet was frozen on dry ice and then stored at -80°C. Sample.Title: 20160714-G2p-R1

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: 1-3 x107 CDK1as wild type cells/sample were treated with 1NM-PP1 (final concentration: 2 μ M) for 10 h, after which over 95% were in G2 (Fig.S14). G2-arrested cells were collected prior to 1NM-PP1-washout. To release cells from the G2-arrest, 1NM-PP1 was washed out by collecting the cells by centrifugation (1300 rpm, 2-5 min, depending on the volume of cultured cells, at RT) and suspending the cells in fresh media (10-20 ml). Cells were washed twice over a period of 10-20 min (depending on the number of cultures being processed simultaneously). These cells were suspended in fresh media and divided into flasks (one flask for each time point, typically 1-3 x107 cells per flask) and returned to the incubator (counted asTime=0 min). At each time point (0, 2.5, 5, 7.5, 10, 30, 45, 60, 75 min), a flask was removed from the incubator and cells with media were transferred to a 50 ml falcon tube, which was topped up with fresh media to 45 ml.
Channel.Extract-Protocol: 3 ml of 16% formaldehyde solution was added to the 50 ml tube to obtain a final concentration of 1%. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 10 min for cross-linking. 2.5 ml of 2.5 M glycine was then added to the tube to stop cross-linking. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 5 min, and then on ice for at least 15 min. Subsequently, cells were collected by centrifugation (2000 rpm, 10 min), and the cell pellet was frozen on dry ice and then stored at -80°C. HiC was performed as in Belton et. al, 2012, http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1046202312001168?via%3Dihub

Channel.Treatment-Protocol: 1-3 x107 CDK1as wild type cells/sample were treated with 1NM-PP1 (final concentration: 2 μ M) for 10 h, after which over 95% were in G2 (Fig.S14). G2-arrested cells were collected prior to 1NM-PP1-washout. To release cells from the G2-arrest, 1NM-PP1 was washed out by collecting the cells by centrifugation (1300 rpm, 2-5 min, depending on the volume of cultured cells, at RT) and suspending the cells in fresh media (10-20 ml). Cells were washed twice over a period of 10-20 min (depending on the number of cultures being processed simultaneously). These cells were suspended in fresh media and divided into flasks (one flask for each time point, typically 1-3 x107 cells per flask) and returned to the incubator (counted asTime=0 min). At each time point (0, 2.5, 5, 7.5, 10, 30, 45, 60, 75 min), a flask was removed from the incubator and cells with media were transferred to a 50 ml falcon tube, which was topped up with fresh media to 45 ml.
Channel.Extract-Protocol: 3 ml of 16% formaldehyde solution was added to the 50 ml tube to obtain a final concentration of 1%. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 10 min for cross-linking. 2.5 ml of 2.5 M glycine was then added to the tube to stop cross-linking. The content was quickly mixed by inverting the tube twice and left at RT for 5 min, and then on ice for at least 15 min. Subsequently, cells were collected by centrifugation (2000 rpm, 10 min), and the cell pellet was frozen on dry ice and then stored at -80°C. HiC was performed as in Belton et. al, 2012, http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1046202312001168?via%3Dihub

Sample.Data-Processing: cMapping Pipeline is publicly availalble at https://github.com/dekkerlab/cMapping This pipeline is describe in the publication by Lajoie et. Al., http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1046202314003582?via%3Dihub Cooler files were obtained from validpairs files. cooler is publicly available at https://github.com/mirnylab/cooler Genome_build: galGal5 Genome_build: hg19 Supplementary_files_format_and_content: validpairs This format gives a list of the interacting pairs of loci in the genome, cooler: chromosome interractions in sparse form. For details, refer to https://github.com/mirnylab/cooler

Series.Title: Mitotic chromosomes fold by condensin-dependent helical winding of chromatin loop arrays
Series.Summary: Mitotic chromosome morphogenesis occurs through condensin-mediated disassembly of the interphase conformation and formation of extended prophase loop arrays that then shorten by condensin-dependent helical winding
